title: Need help in deciding to buy the Sharp NERV edition SH 06D
display_title: Need help in deciding to buy the Sharp NERV edition SH 06D
wikitext:
[]: Need help in deciding to buy the Sharp NERV edition SH-06D             Quote Post by LukeG » Thu Nov 14, 2013 3:22 am So I've decided to buy a new phone and while going through the different android phones on the market I accidentally found the NERV SH-06D. Researching about the phone I found that its limited edition and it has a custom UI with lots of different special features, also I found that its costly to buy £437 ($700) Problem I'm facing is whether or not to buy it. I see this really badass Phone which makes a big fan of the series like me to really want it but on the other hand it is a bit over priced, I know its limited edition and all that but the specs of the phone seem outdated today and the price I'm paying I could buy a higher end phone. While all this may seem like a good reason to not buy it, I still really want it and stuck between going with this one and feeling happy that I've got something Eva themed or going with just a normal android phone. (The Phone is Unlocked) Are the specs of the Phone still good enough for the years to come? And what do you think I should do? Lastly is Android 4.0 good enough to stick with for a couple of years? (Just in case you haven't heard of the phone here some links) http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LxYgIK3pfko Phone Specs http://gadgetauto.com/sharp-sh-06d-nerv-specifications.html

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Nov 14, 2013 3:48 am My advice : don't waste $700 in an outdated phone just because it have a NERV logo and a Eva-themed UI. Use that money to buy a good phone, then search the Internet to buy a NERV themed phone shell and download a custom NERV-themed UI, I'm sure there are plenty of them somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by LukeG » Thu Nov 14, 2013 4:37 am ElMariachi wrote: My advice : don't waste $700 in an outdated phone just because it have a NERV logo and a Eva-themed UI. Use that money to buy a good phone, then search the Internet to buy a NERV themed phone shell and download a custom NERV-themed UI, I'm sure there are plenty of them somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Nov 14, 2013 11:25 am You didn't mention the language, can be switched to English or is the whole thing Japanese only? Just got a smartphone myself so I'm not familiar with this model. I went with a Galaxy S3 that is $20 after rebate and that is coming from an Eva nut. You could also find it cheaper and pricier on Yahoo Japan Auctions (here's a translated version ) and get it from there. If you really really want to still get it and need some help doing so from Japan, there are deputy services that will handle that since most Japanese websites/sellers won't ship overseas. I also know someone who has helped me get stuff from YJA and the Evastore I can put you in touch with.

[]:             Quote Post by Stikuru » Thu Nov 14, 2013 12:36 pm Well, I've been pretty happy with it so far. Granted i bought it purely because i'm a fan of the series and already had the previous Evangelion Sharp phone as well, so I wasn't trying to buy some top of the line phone for personal use to begin with. I've really enjoyed the 3d display on the phone, and getting to watch an Evangelion trailer in 3D was exciting, I can only imagine how great the series would be if it would have all been done in 3D like the video on the phone. Some drawbacks to the phone are that the tv function of it doesn't really work outside of Japan of course, and the sound quality could be a lot better. The sound could easily be fixed by hooking up something externally of course though. Oh, another little pet peeve is the chrome backing. It doesn't look good at all, and fingerprints show up on it like crazy. I just stick with the other cover that is provided with it. I can't really speak for the service or reliability of the phone because I do not use it as an actual phone at all. All i can say is that i've put a prepaid card in it before and have sent a text to myself using it. And in answer to the other question from AR-99, the phone has both Japanese and english settings, and the English is all pretty great throughout. So i guess in summary all I can say is that i've personally loved having the phone, but that my purpose for owning the phone is probably different than what you want to use it for. You might want to consider something different, and like someone else said just get a UI and custom skin/backing/faceplate/whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by LukeG » Thu Nov 14, 2013 2:29 pm Thanks for the help everyone! I'm going to go with the Nexus 5 as getting the NERV SH-06D just as a normal phone is not worth it for me really when I think about it. Unless you're a collector then go for it. I can download the system files from a site in China which contains all the features but the problem is risking a Phone by playing around with its system files and waiting over a day for a 500mb file with 5kb download speed (Why I mentioned where the site is from)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Nov 15, 2013 5:49 am DeeRez bought one and posted a bit about it here: http://forum.evageeks.org/post/552742/The-Gainax-Cash-Cow-or-Why-The-F-and-Did-I-Buy-That/#552742 May find it useful if you're thinking about importing one.

Researching about the phone I found that its limited edition and it has a custom UI with lots of different special features, also I found that its costly to buy £437 ($700&#4
